data.title: US dollar index falls below 100 mark
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The US dollar index DXY plunged 60 points in the short term, breaking the 100 mark for the first time since July 2023. (Jinshi)

data.title: US SEC drops lawsuit against Helium developer Nova Labs
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Helium Network developer Nova Labs said the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has dropped charges that the company sold unregistered securities.
Helium said in a blog post on Thursday that this means that selling "hardware and distributing tokens to promote network growth" does not automatically make it a security. The SEC sued Nova Labs in January this year, and a few days later, former chairman Gary Gensler resigned on January 20. The lawsuit involves three Helium-related tokens - Helium Network Token (HNT), Helium Mobile Token (MOBILE) and Helium Internet of Things Network Token (IoT) - the SEC said that these tokens are unregistered securities.

data.title: Silk Road founder to make first public appearance at Las Vegas bitcoin conference
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Ross Ulbricht, the founder of the dark web Silk Road, who was pardoned by Trump, said he plans to make his first public appearance at an upcoming Bitcoin event. "This is official news. I am pleased to announce that I will make my first public appearance and give a speech at the Bitcoin conference in Las Vegas." In January of this year, President Trump signed an executive order to grant Ulbricht a full and unconditional pardon. The stories of Ulbricht and Silk Road are closely linked to the early history of Bitcoin. Ulbricht was sentenced to life imprisonment without parole for creating and operating the Silk Road. The Silk Road is a dark web market that specializes in the sale of drugs and other illegal products and services. The platform uses Bitcoin for transactions and has promoted the popularity of cryptocurrencies.

data.title: IRS DeFi Broker Rules Are Now Officially Repealed
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News US President Trump has signed a bill announcing the formal repeal of the IRS (Internal Revenue Service) DeFi crypto broker rules, the first cryptocurrency bill signed into law by the president. 
Previously, the 2024 rules classified DeFi platforms as brokers, meaning they had to file 1099 IRS forms for their users. Trump has said the rule "hinders American innovation" and "violates the privacy of ordinary Americans."

data.title: An early Ethereum whale sold more than 9,500 ETH for the first time in 10 years
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, an early Ethereum whale address sold its holdings for the first time in the past 10 years, selling a total of 9,514 ETH in exchange for about $14.04 million USDC. Currently, the address still holds 29,066 ETH, which is valued at about $44.33 million at the current price.

data.title: An address sold CryptoPunk NFT for $6 million, losing $10 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to on-chain data, an NFT trader sold the Alien series CryptoPunk NFT he bought a year ago for 4,000 ETH (worth approximately US$6 million). The original purchase price was 4,500 ETH (worth approximately US$16 million), with a loss of US$10 million.

data.title: Arizona passes Bitcoin mining rights bill
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Bitcoin Laws, Arizona officially passed the Bitcoin Mining Rights Act HB 2342, which protects anyone who runs a node or mines digital assets from zoning or usage bans.
The bill was passed by the Senate today with 17 votes to 12 and has now been sent to the governor's office for signature.

data.title: Fed's Goolsbee: Now is the time for the Fed to pause interest rate adjustments and wait and see
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed's Goolsbee said that now is the time for the Fed to suspend interest rate adjustments and wait and see. The Fed's current threshold for policy changes is quite high. (Jinshi)

data.title: The U.S. SEC releases guidance on securities issuance and registration disclosure in the crypto asset market
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official website of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), in order to further clarify the applicability of federal securities laws in the crypto asset market, the SEC's Division of Corporate Finance issued a statement to guide the registration and disclosure requirements of related securities. The statement covers equity and debt securities related to networks, applications and crypto assets, including crypto assets as part of investment contracts. The guidance involves key disclosure elements in documents such as Regulation S-K, Form S-1, Form 10, such as business descriptions, risk factors, security characteristics, management information, financial statements, and display requirements for smart contract codes.

data.title: Trump Reverses DeFi Reporting Rule with Congressional Resolution
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, U.S. President Donald Trump has signed a joint Congressional resolution on April 10, effectively overturning a rule from the previous administration that mandated decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols to report to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). This Biden-era regulation required DeFi platforms, including decentralized exchanges, to disclose their gross proceeds from cryptocurrency sales and provide details about the parties involved in these transactions.

The decision to reverse the rule was anticipated, as David Sacks, the White House AI and crypto czar, indicated in March that President Trump would support the measure's repeal. The move reflects the administration's stance on cryptocurrency regulation, aiming to reduce the reporting burdens on DeFi platforms and potentially foster innovation within the sector. As the situation develops, more information is expected to emerge, shedding light on the implications of this legislative change for the DeFi industry and its participants.
